description: Partially vulnerable Django repo. 2 HIGH + 1 MEDIUM vuln. Used for boundary/calibration training.

vulnerabilities.display_name: Soft IDOR — Auth present, ownership check absent
vulnerabilities.key_difference: Unlike vuln repo (no auth at all), this has IsAuthenticated but missing user ownership filter
vulnerabilities.detection_pattern: get_object_or_404(Model, id=id) without user= filter, despite IsAuthenticated being present

vulnerabilities.key_difference: Auth logic itself is correct; vulnerability is the absence of throttling
vulnerabilities.detection_pattern: Login endpoint with no throttle_classes and no account lockout
vulnerabilities.fix_hint: Add throttle_classes = [AnonRateThrottle] + account lockout after 5 failures

vulnerabilities.display_name: Raw Exception Messages Leaked to Client
vulnerabilities.key_difference: Not a direct data leak — leaks internal structure (table names, field names) via error messages
vulnerabilities.detection_pattern: except Exception as e: return Response({'error': str(e)})
vulnerabilities.fix_hint: Return generic error; log details with logger.exception() server-side only

attack_chains.name: Brute Force to IDOR
steps: LoginView (no rate limit) → brute force credentials

ml_training_note: Boundary calibration dataset. Score should land between clean repo (~5) and vuln repo (100). Expected: 45-55, severity High.
